text:
Once upon a time, in a small village nestled within a whispering forest, there lived a wise old owl named Oblio. Oblio wasn’t your typical nocturnal bird; he didn’t swoop overhead or chitter about the affairs of mortals. Instead, he spent his days perched on ancient oaks, listening to the rustling leaves and distant creek.

One bright spring morning, feeling a bit lonely despite his wise solitude, Oblio decided to venture out from his usual perch. The village was celebrating the annual Spring Festival, and he had always looked forward to this occasion, not as a festival of loud noises, but as a festival of gentle songs and laughter that echoed the harmony of nature.

As he flew, his keen eyes noticed something odd. Various villagers, including the youngest of them, Flickerfoot, were decorating their homes with feathers and flowers, but there was a particular item causing concern—one giant sunflower stalk, towering higher than all the trees.
